subject: [PATCH v2] mm/percpu, memcontrol: Per-memcg-lruvec percpu accounting

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in exporting mod_memcg_lruvec_state, which was unnecessary for this patch and only leftover from another series. The author apologized for the 'noise' and promised no further changes are needed.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Matthew, thank you for reviewing my patch!

Whoops, no module uses this symbol in this patch. I was working on a similar
patch series (accounting zswap statistics on a per-lruvec level [1]) where I did
the same thing, and needed to export this so that using it in zsmalloc
wouldn't break if zram was built as a module.

Obviously there's no need for exporting for this patch but I forgot to drop
that part when copying this over from that series. Sorry for the noise!

Thanks again for catching this. I hope you have a great day!
Joshua

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260311195153.4013476-10-joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com/

2026-04-03.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory accounting system to provide more detailed information about per-memcg-lruvec (per-cgroup, per-node, and per-LRU vector) percpu allocations. It does this by converting a certain type of memory tracking from a per-memcg level to a per-node level, allowing for better visibility into memory pressure on multi-NUMA systems. The patch also introduces separate accounting for node-level statistics and memcg-lruvec statistics, which requires exposing a new function to be used outside of the memory control module. This change improves the kernel's ability to track memory usage at a finer granularity, particularly for percpu allocations, but has a small memory overhead and negligible performance impact.

The patch also introduces separate accounting for node-level statistics and memcg-lruvec statistics, which requires exposing a new function to be used outside of the memory control module.
